About what are they asking one another?
Is it about the awesome tiding
that they are in utter disagreement?Footnote-1
No indeed;Footnote-2 soon will they come to know;Footnote-3
again, no indeed; soon will they come to know.
Have We not spread the earth like a bed,Footnote-4
and fixed the mountains like pegs,Footnote-5
and created you in pairs (as men and women),Footnote-6
and made your sleep a means of repose,Footnote-7
and made the night a covering,
and made the day to seek livelihood,Footnote-8
and built above you seven strong firmaments,Footnote-9
and placed therein a hot, shining lamp,Footnote-10
and sent down abundant water from the clouds
so that We may thereby bring forth grain and vegetation,
and gardens dense with foliage?Footnote-11
Surely the Day of Judgement has an appointed time;
the Day when the Trumpet shall be blown, and you will come forth in multitudes;Footnote-12
and when the sky shall be opened up and will become all doors;
and the mountains will be set in motion and become a mirage.Footnote-13
Surely the Hell is an ambush,Footnote-14
a resort for the rebellious;
therein they shall abide for ages,Footnote-15
they shall taste in it no coolness, nor any pleasant drink
save boiling water and wash of the wounds;Footnote-16
a befitting recompense for their deeds.
For indeed they did not look forward to any reckoning,
and roundly denied Our Signs as false.Footnote-17
And everything have We recorded in a Book.Footnote-18
So taste (the fruit of your deeds). We shall only increase your torment.
Surely the state of triumph awaits the God-fearing:Footnote-19
gardens and vineyards,
and youthful maidens of like age,Footnote-20
and an overflowing cup.
Therein they shall hear no idle talk, nor any falsehood;Footnote-21
a recompense from your Lord and an ample rewardFootnote-22
from the Lord of the heavens and the earth and of that which is between them; the Most Merciful Lord before Whom none dare utter a word.Footnote-23
The Day when the SpiritFootnote-24 and the angels are ranged row on row. None shall speak save he whom the Merciful Lord will permit; and he too will speak what is right.Footnote-25
That Day is sure to come. So let him who will seek a resort with his Lord.

Lo! We warn you of a chastisement near at hand;Footnote-26 the Day when a man will look on what his own hands have sent forth, and the unbelievers shall say: “Oh would that I were utter dust.”Footnote-27
